references.text: Buysen, JRM. 1993. Alliaceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 11: 375-384
Geerinck, DJL. 1993. Amaryllidaceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 11: 353-373

Vera Cruz, JRA, RP Gentallan Jr, MCB Bartolome, IA Calimpang, KJO Quiñones, MM Manting, RE Madayag, AG Lalusin, JE Hernandez, MCC Manuel, LE Endonela & TH Borromeo. 2023. Taxonomic evidences supporting the establishment of ‘sibujing', a traditional condiment crop from the Philippines, as Allium chinense G.Don. Nordic Journal of Botany e04054.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 31 October 2023

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 295 ; Buysen, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 381. = Allium porrum L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 295.
species.distribution: Africa, Europe, Middle East, W Asia.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 300 ; Buysen, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 381. ≠ Allium ascalonicum auct. non L. ; Ochse & Bakh., Veget. Dutch East Indies (1931) 441.
species.notes: Onion, shallot. The common onion is not a wild species, but in its primary center of diversity, C Asia, several related wild species occur. A secondary center of diversity is the Near East and the Mediterranean, a rich diversity is also found in India.

species.citation: G.Don , Mem. Wern. Nat. Hist. Soc. 6 (1827) 83 ; Buysen, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 381; Vera Cruz et al., Nordic J. Bot. e04054 (2023) 1.
L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 301 ; Buysen, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 383.
L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 297 ; Buysen, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 383.
Rottler ex Spreng. , Syst. Veg., 16th Ed. 2 (1825) 38 ; Buysen, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 383.
L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 293 ; Steiner, Philipp. Ornam. Pl. (1960) 136; Geerinck, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 371.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 14 June 2026

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 292 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 147; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 210; Geerinck, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 359; Romeroso et al., Asian J. Biol. Life Sc. 10 (2021) 437; Cañeda et al., Davao Research J. 16 (2025) 91; Type: from India, Malabar Coast and Sri Lanka in LINN. var. asiaticum. Distribution: LUZON, MINDANAO, MINDORO, PALAWAN. Notes: Along sandy seashores, sometimes planted inland. 0(-?)m. Native.
species.distribution: Australia, Bismarck Arch, Borneo, Cambodia, India, Indian Ocean, Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Madura, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, New Caledonia, New Guinea, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Singapore, Solomon Isls, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. BORACAY (photos), CAGAYAN DE SULU, DINAGAT (photos), HOMONHON, HUNDRED ISLS (photos), LUZON: Aurora (photos), Bataan, Camarines Sur, Ifugao, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, NCR, Pangasinan, MINDANAO: Davao Occidental, Davao del Sur, Zamboanga, MINDORO, PALAWAN, SIARGAO.

species.citation: G.Mey. ex C.Presl , Reliq. Haenk. 1 (1827) 120 ; Naves, Novis. App. (1880) 255; Baker, Handb. Amaryl. (1888) 81; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 210; Geerinck, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 361; Type: Haenke, Luzon. = Crinum cumingii Baker , Gard. Chron., n.s. 16 (1881) 72 ; Handb. Amaryll. (1888) 81; Vidal, Phan. Cuming. Philip. (1885) 152; Rev. Pl. Vasc. Filip. (1886) 275; Type: Cuming 1382.
species.distribution: New Guinea, Philippines, Sumatra. LUZON: Albay, Benguet (photos), Laguna, Nueva Ecija, Rizal (photos), MINDANAO, MINDORO.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 291 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 148; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 210; Steiner, Philip. Ornam. Pl. (1960) 137; Geerinck, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 361.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Bangladesh, China, India, Laos, Myanmar, Nicobar Isls, Sri Lanka, Thailand, Vietnam.
species.notes: Cultivated as an ornamental and scarcely naturalized.

species.citation: (L.) L. , Syst. Nat., 12th Ed. (1767) 236 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 148; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 211; Geerinck, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 361; ≡ Amaryllis zeylanica L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 293.
species.distribution: India, Indian Ocean, Sri Lanka. LUZON: Abra (photos).
species.notes: Cultivated as an ornamental and naturalized.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 19 July 2022

species.citation: Linden ex Planch. , Ill. Hort. 28 (1881) 30 ; Geerinck, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 371. ≠ Eucharis × grandiflora auct. non Planch. & Linden ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 149; EPFP 1 (1922) 211.
species.notes: Occurs in the Philippines only as a cultivated ornamental. Amazon lily.

species.citation: Planch. & Linden , Fl. Serres Jard. Eur. 9 (1854) 255 ; Steiner, Philip. Ornam. Pl. (1960) 137. = Eucharis mastersii Baker , Bot. Mag. 111 (1885) t.6831 ; Geerinck, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 372.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 28 August 2022

species.citation: (L.) Herb. , Appendix (Herbert) (1821) 31 ; Geerinck, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 372; ≡ Amaryllis reginae L. , Syst. Nat., 10th Ed. 2 (1759) 977. = Amaryllis atamasco Blanco ; Fl. Filip. (1837) 254, nom. illeg., non L. ≠ Hippeastrum miniatum auct. non (Ruiz & Pav.) Herb. ; Merr., EPFP 1 (1922) 212.
species.distribution: Neotropics. LUZON: La Union, Laguna, Quezon, Rizal, MINDANAO, PANAY, SIASI.
species.notes: Cultivated for ornamental purposes, but apparently naturalized under coconut groves, etc.

species.citation: (Lam.) Voss , Vilm. Blumengärtn., 3rd Ed. 1 (1895) 1033; ≡ Hippeastrum puniceum (Lam.) Kuntze , Revis. Gen. Pl. 2 (1891) 703 ; Geerinck, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 372; ≡ Amaryllis punicea Lam. , Encycl. (Lamarck) 1 (1783) 122. = Hippeastrum equestre (Aiton) Herb. , Appendix (Herbert) (1821) 31 ; Steiner, Philipp. Ornam. Pl. (1960) 137; ≡ Amaryllis equestris Aiton , Hort. Kew., 1st Ed. 1 (1789) 417, nom. illeg.
(L'Hér.) Herb. , Bot. Mag. 51 (1824) t.2475 ; Steiner, Philipp. Ornam. Pl. (1960) 137; Geerinck, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 372; ≡ Amaryllis reticulata L'Hér. , Sert. Angl. (1789) 12.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 13 October 2017

species.citation: (Jacq.) Salisb. , Trans. Hort. Soc. London 1 (1812) 338 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 211; Geerinck, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 362; ≡ Pancratium littorale Jacq. , Select. Stirp. Amer. Hist. (1763) 99; Type: from West Indies.
species.notes: Cultivated and naturalized in the Philippines. Cultivated around towns, occasional in waste places, along seashores and in moist sandy places at low elevation.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 290 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 148; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 212; Geerinck, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 363; Type: Probably from Sri Lanka, but perhaps of garden origin.
species.distribution: India, Sri Lanka. BILIRAN, JOLO (photos), LUZON: Camarines, La Union, PALAWAN.
species.notes: Coconut and abaca plantations, grasslands, thickets, also cultivated in gardens, up to 600m.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 316 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 148; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 212.
species.notes: Cultivated for its fragrant flowers but nowhere naturalized in the Philippines. Tuberose or azucena.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 13 October 2017

species.citation: (L.) Herb. , Appendix (Herbert) (1821) 42 ; Geerinck, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 366; ≡ Eurycles amboinensis (L.) Lindl. , Encycl. Pl. (Loudon) (1829) 242 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 149; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 211; ≡ Pancratium amboinense L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 291.
species.distribution: Bismarck Arch, Lesser Sunda Isls, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi, Thailand. CAGAYAN DE SULU, LUZON: Cavite, Laguna, Rizal, MINDORO, PALAWAN.
species.notes: Seashores and rocky places, secondary forests, often cultivated as an ornamental, apparently never far from human settlements and hence possibly introduced. 0-500m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 22 October 2020

species.citation: (Martyn) Raf. , Fl. Tellur. 4 (1838) 19 ; Geerinck, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 373; ≡ Haemanthus multiflorus Martyn , Gard. Dict., 9th Ed. (1795) s.n. ; Steiner, Philipp. Ornam. Pl. (1960) 137.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 22 October 2020

species.citation: Lindl. , Bot. Reg. 10 (1824) t.821 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 147; Steiner, Philipp. Ornam. Pl. (1960) 138; Geerinck, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 373; ≡ Atamasco rosea (Lindl.) Greene , Pittonia 3 (1897) 188 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 210.
species.notes: Widely cultivated as an ornamental in and about towns, sometimes persisting from previous cultivation but hardly naturalized.

species.citation: (L.) Herb. , Appendix (Herbert) (1821) 36 ; Steiner, Philipp. Ornam. Pl. (1960) 138; Geerinck, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 373; ≡ Amaryllis atamasco L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 292.
(Lindl.) Herb. , Bot. Mag. 53 (1826) t.2607 ; Geerinck, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 373; ≡ Amaryllis candida Lindl. , Bot. Reg. 9 (1823) t.724.
species.notes: Note by P.B. Pelser (28-Aug-20): Conklin & Del Rosario 116 (PNH 72636), L, ?PNH (cultivated in Mountain Province) represents this species in the Philippines.

species.citation: Herb. , Bot. Mag. 52 (1825) t.2594 ; Steiner, Philipp. Ornam. Pl. (1960) 138; Geerinck, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 11 (1993) 373.
